title: श्री तुलसी विवाह स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री तुलसी विवाह स्तोत्रम् ॥

नमस्तुलसि कल्याणि नमो विष्णुप्रिये शुभे।
नमो मोक्षप्रदे देवि नमः सम्पत्प्रदायिनि॥ १॥

तुलसि श्रीसखि शुभे पापहारिणि पुण्यदे।
नमस्ते नारदनुते नारायणमनःप्रिये॥ २॥

तुलस्यमृतजन्मासि सदा त्वं केशवप्रिया।
केशवार्थे चिनोमि त्वां वरदा भव शोभने॥ ३॥

त्वदङ्गसम्भवैः पत्रैः पूजयामि यथा हरिम्।
तथा कुरु पवित्रां मां शरणागतवत्सले॥ ४॥

चयनोद्भवदुःखं च यद्‌ हृदि तव वर्तते।
तत्‌ क्षमस्व मया देवि नमामि चरणौ तव॥ ५॥

विष्णुभक्तिप्रदे देवि शापमुक्तासि भूतले।
शालग्रामे सदा तिष्ठ तुलसि त्वं नमोऽस्तु ते॥ ६॥

धन्या तुलसि गोविन्दचरणप्रेमदायिनी।
यस्या दर्शनमात्रेण पापानां नशते चयः॥ ७॥

तुलस्याः पल्लवं विष्णोः शिरस्यारोपितं यदा।
तदा कोटिसहस्रेण ज्ञेयं दत्तं फलं भवेत्॥ ८॥

तुलसी श्रीमहालक्ष्मीर्विद्या विद्या यशस्विनी।
धर्म्या धर्मानना देवी देवदेवमनःप्रिया॥ ९॥

लक्ष्मीप्रिये लक्ष्मिरूपे लक्ष्मि त्वां शरणं गता।
कृष्णभक्तिप्रदे देवि सत्यवत्यै नमो नमः॥ १०॥

विवाहे च यथा लक्ष्म्या विष्णुना सह सङ्गमः।
तथा तुलसिविष्ण्वोश्च विवाहो मङ्गलप्रदः॥ ११॥

तुलसीविवाहस्तोत्रं यः पठेत् भक्तिसंयुतः।
सौभाग्यं लभते नित्यं विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥ १२॥

॥ इति श्री तुलसी विवाह स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Tulsi Vivah Stotram ||

Namas-Tulasi kalyani namo Vishnu-priye Shubhe |
Namo moksha-prade Devi namah sampat-pradayini || 1 ||

Tulasi Shri-sakhi shubhe papa-harini punya-de |
Namaste Narada-nute Narayana-manah-priye || 2 ||

Tulasy-amrita-janmasi sada tvam Keshava-priya |
Keshav-arthe chinomi tvam varada bhava shobhane || 3 ||

Tvad-anga-sambhavaih patraih pujayami yatha Harim |
Tatha kuru pavitram mam sharanagata-vatsale || 4 ||

Chayanod-bhava-duhkham cha yad hridi tava vartate |
Tat kshamasva maya Devi namami charanau tava || 5 ||

Vishnu-bhakti-prade Devi shapa-muktasi bhutale |
Shalagrame sada tishtha Tulasi tvam namo-astu te || 6 ||

Dhanya Tulasi Govinda-charana-prema-dayini |
Yasya darshana-matrena papanaam nashate chayah || 7 ||

Tulasyah pallavam Vishnoh shirasy-aropitam yada |
Tada koti-sahasrena gneyam dattam phalam bhavet || 8 ||

Tulasi Shri-Mahalaksmir-vidya vidya yashasvini |
Dharmya dharmanana Devi Deva-deva-manah-priya || 9 ||

Lakshmi-priye Lakshmi-rupe Lakshmi tvam sharanam gata |
Krishna-bhakti-prade Devi Satyavatyai namo namah || 10 ||

Vivahe cha yatha Lakshmya Vishnuna saha sangamah |
Tatha Tulasi-Vishnvosh-cha vivaho mangala-pradah || 11 ||

Tulasi-vivaha-stotram yah pathet bhakti-samyutah |
Saubhagyam labhate nityam Vishnulokam sa gachchhati || 12 ||

|| Iti Shri Tulsi Vivah Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: हे तुलसी! कल्याणी, विष्णुप्रिया, मोक्षप्रदा, सम्पत्प्रदायिनी — आपको नमस्कार। हे श्रीसखी, पापहारिणी, पुण्यदा, नारद द्वारा स्तुत, नारायण को प्रिय — आपको प्रणाम। आप अमृत से उत्पन्न हैं, सदा केशव को प्रिय हैं। आपके पत्तों से हरि की पूजा करता हूँ, मुझे पवित्र कीजिए। आप विष्णुभक्ति प्रदान करने वाली, शापमुक्त होकर भूतल पर प्रकट हैं, शालग्राम के साथ सदा विराजमान हैं। आपके दर्शनमात्र से पापों का नाश होता है। तुलसी-विष्णु का विवाह लक्ष्मी-विष्णु के विवाह के समान मङ्गलप्रद है।
benefits: Blesses with marital harmony and conjugal bliss
Purifies sins through the sacred Tulsi worship
Invokes the blessing of saubhagya (good fortune) to married women
Traditionally associated with prosperity and auspiciousness to the household
Leads to Vishnuloka for sincere devotees
description: Tulsi Vivah Stotram (श्री तुलसी विवाह स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Vishnu. This sacred composition is sourced from Padma Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to lead to Vishnuloka for sincere devotees, bestow saubhagya (good fortune) to married women, and purify sins through the sacred Tulsi worship. This sacred text is ideally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and Monday (Somvar) and during Tulsi Vivah, Kartik Purnima, and Devuthani Ekadashi for enhanced blessings. Comprising 12 verses, it can be completed in approximately 8 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Tulsi Vivah Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Tulsi Vivah Stotram in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn for Tulsi-Shaligram wedding ceremony and marital blessings.

seoFaqs.question: What is the scriptural basis for the Tulsi Vivah Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Tulsi Vivah Stotram is rooted in the Brahma Vaivarta Purana, which preserves the most extensive narrative of Tulsi as the goddess Vrinda, daughter of Dharma and consort of the demon king Jalandhara. When Lord Vishnu, in disguise, broke her chastity-borne protection of her husband, she cursed him to become the Shaligram stone and was herself transformed into the sacred Tulsi plant, who would forever be married to Vishnu in his Shaligram form. Verses praising this union form the basis of the Tulsi Vivah Stotram, recited annually on Kartik Shukla Ekadashi to Dvadashi when devotees ceremonially marry the Tulsi plant to Shaligram. (Sources: Greenmesg; Mantra Shlokas Madhuri; Hindu Gallery.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is traditionally credited with composing the popular Tulasi Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The widely circulated Tulasi Stotram beginning Jagaddhatri namastubhyam is attributed to Sage Pundarika, a saint associated with Thirukadalmallai (Mahabalipuram) in Tamil Nadu. He is venerated in Sri Vaishnava lineage as one of the early devotees who composed Sanskrit hymns to Tulsi as the most beloved consort of Vishnu. The Brahma Vaivarta Purana itself contains additional Tulsi praise verses spoken by Brahma, Narada and the Gandharvas, but the Pundarika composition is the one most commonly recited during Tulsi Vivah and daily Tulsi puja in households across India. (Sources: Hindupedia Thulasi Stotram; Shlokam.org.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Tulsi Vivah ceremony performed at home?
seoFaqs.answer: The household ceremony begins on Prabodhini Ekadashi when the Tulsi plant in the courtyard tulsi-vrindavan is bathed, decorated with a red sari, sindoor, bangles and a small mangalsutra, and placed beside a Shaligram or a small Krishna murti representing the bridegroom. A canopy of sugarcane stalks is erected over the plant. The Tulsi Vivah Stotram is recited along with the standard Vivah-mantras adapted from grihya-sutra wedding rites, followed by mangalashtaka, kanyadaan, the sapta-padi performed symbolically around the plant, and aarti. Prasad of fresh fruits, amla and sweets is distributed. The ceremony formally ends the chaturmas period and signals the start of the Hindu wedding season. (Sources: knramesh blog; Drikpanchang; Mantra Shlokas Madhuri.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the marriage performed specifically on Kartik Shukla Ekadashi to Dvadashi?
seoFaqs.answer: Kartik Shukla Ekadashi is celebrated as Prabodhini Ekadashi, the day Vishnu awakens from his four-month yoga-nidra of chaturmas. Hindu tradition holds that no auspicious vivaha-samskara may be performed during chaturmas; the awakening of Vishnu therefore marks the reopening of the wedding calendar, and his own symbolic marriage to Tulsi as Shaligram on Dvadashi is treated as the muhurta-inaugurating event for the entire winter wedding season. Tulsi Vivah is observed on Dvadashi in most North Indian and Maharashtrian traditions, while some southern almanacs extend the observance up to Kartik Purnima five days later. (Sources: Mantra Shlokas Madhuri; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What spiritual benefits do the scriptures associate with reciting the Tulsi Vivah Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Verses within the stotram and its phalashruti promise multiple benefits: protection from untimely death, removal of marital obstacles for unmarried daughters, blessings for marital harmony for married couples, conferral of progeny, washing away of accumulated sins on a par with the merit of performing kanyadaan, and the highest reward of vaikuntha-loka after death. Reciting it daily before the household tulsi plant, particularly during Kartik month, is said to multiply the merit of every other puja performed in the home. The Brahma Vaivarta Purana specifically states that one who arranges Tulsi Vivah in a lifetime obtains the merit of giving away one hundred daughters in marriage. (Sources: Greenmesg Tulasi Stotram; Hindu Gallery; Shlokam.org.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the relationship between Tulsi Vivah Stotram and the Tulsi Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The two texts are companion compositions used in Tulsi-worship but serve different ritual functions. The Tulsi Vivah Stotram is a praise-hymn celebrating the bridal union of Tulsi-Vrinda with Vishnu-Shaligram and is recited within the Vivah ceremony. The Tulsi Kavacham, recorded in the Brahmanda Purana, is a protective armor-prayer that envelops the devotee with Tulsi's protective grace, with each verse assigning her protection over a particular limb or direction. Devotees often chant the Kavacham daily for protection and the Vivah Stotram annually during Kartik for the marital and progeny benefits. (Sources: ioustotra Tulsi Kavacham; Hindupedia.)
